Feature.Description: Ardea-specific checks for whether the DENSO robot is at a known named pose. The current joint angles (CurJnt) are read over b-CAP and compared element-wise against the reference pose from the motion configuration on the first six axes (J1..J6) within a small tolerance. These commands only read; they do not move the robot. Carriage movement is permitted when the robot is at either the base pose or the retract pose.

Command.Description: Return whether the robot is at the base pose (home/origin position), i.e. CurJnt matches the configured base_pose joint angles within tolerance on the first six axes.

Response.Description: True if the robot is at the base pose.

Command.Description: Return whether the robot is at the retract pose (retract position), i.e. CurJnt matches the configured retract_pose joint angles within tolerance on the first six axes.

Response.Description: True if the robot is at the retract pose.

DefinedExecutionError.Description: Failed to connect to or communicate with the robot controller over b-CAP.
Failed to acquire the robot or read the current joint angles.
The current joint angles have fewer axes than required to compare against the reference pose.
